title: stenochlaena marginata
intro: Stenochlaena marginata, also known as the Australian leaved fern, is a species of fern in the family Blechnaceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing skin irritations. In herbal preparations, Stenochlaena marginata is often used in teas, infusions, and poultices, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
